title: How To Make a High-Deductible Health Plan Work for You
summary: Lower premiums often mean higher costs when you get sick and need care. Among the ways to plan ahead and soften the financial hit: health savings accounts, which act like a medical piggy bank.

title: Pennsylvania Town Faces Fallout From Trump’s Environmental Rule Rollback
summary: Even as the Trump administration publicly embraces the Make America Healthy Again movement and its ideals about reducing corporate harm to the environment, it has taken steps to stall environmental protections that MAHA followers hold dear.

title: Rovner Recaps Medicaid Cuts’ Impact on Hospitals and Fields Caller Questions on Affordability
summary: KFF Health News chief Washington correspondent Julie Rovner recently made the radio rounds to discuss topical stories. Here’s a collection of her appearances.

title: The Trump Administration Is Seeking Federal Workers’ Sensitive Medical Data. That’s Raising Alarms.
summary: Get our weekly newsletter, The Week in Brief, featuring a roundup of our original coverage, Fridays at 2 p.m. ET.

title: For Many Patients Leaving the ICU, the Struggle Has Only Just Begun
summary: A long stay in intensive care can bring physical, cognitive, and mental health challenges that can take months or longer to resolve.

title: Watch: As AI Makes More Health Coverage Decisions, the Risks to Patients Grow
summary: Major health insurers and even Medicare are using artificial intelligence to make coverage decisions. But class action lawsuits have accused insurers of using AI to wrongfully withhold treatment, and new research illuminates the risks.

title: What the Health? From KFF Health News: Abortion Pills, the Budget, and RFK Jr.
summary: This week, the Trump administration won a court battle to delay a ruling on access to the abortion pill mifepristone, angering its own anti-abortion allies. Meanwhile, the president’s budget arrived on Capitol Hill, where lawmakers are unlikely to agree to its proposed cuts to Health and Human Services programs. Lauren Weber of The Washington Post, Alice Miranda Ollstein of Politico, and Maya Goldman of Axios join KFF Health News’ Julie Rovner to discuss these stories and more.

title: Farm Bureau Health Plans Beat the ACA on Prices With an Age-Old Tactic: Rejecting Sick People
summary: Fourteen states now allow health coverage through state farm bureaus. Though they generally share many features of Affordable Care Act marketplace plans, they aren’t insurance. Neither are they typically subject to federal or state health insurance requirements, and the benefits may be less generous or predictable than those of Obamacare plans.

title: States Face Another Challenge With Medicaid Work Rules: Staffing Shortages
summary: Some states already don’t have enough staff to quickly process Medicaid applications and answer enrollees’ phone calls. Researchers say they may not be prepared to handle new Medicaid work rules, predicting people will lose coverage as a result.

title: Urgent Care Clinics Move To Fill Abortion Care Gaps in Rural Areas
summary: When the only clinic that offered abortions in Michigan’s rural Upper Peninsula closed, an urgent care facility stepped in to fill the gap. Now, others are considering similar moves as brick-and-mortar clinics close in blue states.
